name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If aligned(addr, 0) is provable, the spec is unsound on division-by-zero (nat % 0 is unspecified in SMT)

name: upper_vaddr_equals_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_upper_vaddr_equals_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: upper_vaddr equalling base would mean a layer maps zero address space, contradicting inv's positive entry_size and num_entries

name: entry_next_entry_equal
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_next_entry_equal(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx) == arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: entry_base equalling next_entry_base implies zero-sized entries, violating the positive entry_size invariant

name: well_formed_zero_entries
code:
proof fn phi_4_well_formed_zero_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
    ensures
        d.entries.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A well_formed directory with zero entries would be degenerate; arch.inv() requires num_entries > 0 so entries.len() should be > 0

name: dir_upper_leq_base
code:
proof fn phi_5_dir_upper_leq_base(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.inv(),
    ensures
        d.upper_vaddr() <= d.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: A valid directory's upper_vaddr must exceed base_vaddr to map a non-empty region; if upper ≤ base the address range is empty or inverted
